# Function Reference

All 95 built-in functions of the [Flowfile formula language](index.md). Unless noted, every argument accepts a literal value, a `[column]` reference or a nested expression. Click *Try it ▸* to load an example into the [interactive playground](https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/).

<section class="fn-category" id="logic-nulls" data-cat="logic-nulls">
<h2>Logic &amp; Nulls <span class="fn-count">(13)</span></h2>
<div class="fn-grid">
<article class="fn-card" id="between" data-text="between(value, min_val, max_val) checks if a value is between a minimum and maximum value (inclusive). logic &amp; nulls">
<header><code class="fn-sig">between(value, min_val, max_val)</code><a class="fn-try" href="https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/#ds=employees&amp;expr=between%28%5Bage%5D%2C%2030%2C%2040%29" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Try it ▸</a></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Checks if a value is between a minimum and maximum value (inclusive).</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>between([age], 30, 40)</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <code class="fn-result">true</code><span class="fn-ctx">when [age] is 35</span></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><code>value</code> — The column or value to check</li><li><code>min_val</code> — The column or value used as the minimum (inclusive)</li><li><code>max_val</code> — The column or value used as the maximum (inclusive)</li><li><strong>Returns</strong> — true if the value is between min and max (inclusive), otherwise false</li></ul></details>
</article>
<article class="fn-card" id="coalesce" data-text="coalesce(*values) returns the first non-empty value from a list of values. logic &amp; nulls">
<header><code class="fn-sig">coalesce(*values)</code><a class="fn-try" href="https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/#ds=orders&amp;expr=coalesce%28%5Bdiscount%5D%2C%200%29" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Try it ▸</a></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Returns the first non-empty value from a list of values.</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>coalesce([discount], 0)</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <code class="fn-result">0</code><span class="fn-ctx">when [discount] is empty</span></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><code>*values</code> — The columns or values to check in order</li><li><strong>Returns</strong> — The first non-empty value, or null if all values are empty</li></ul></details>
</article>
<article class="fn-card" id="contains" data-text="contains(text, search_for) checks if some text contains a specific pattern. logic &amp; nulls">
<header><code class="fn-sig">contains(text, search_for)</code><a class="fn-try" href="https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/#ds=orders&amp;expr=contains%28%5Bproduct%5D%2C%20%22Laptop%22%29" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Try it ▸</a></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Checks if some text contains a specific pattern.</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>contains([product], &quot;Laptop&quot;)</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <code class="fn-result">true</code><span class="fn-ctx">when [product] is &quot;Laptop Pro 15&quot;</span></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><code>text</code> — The column or value to search in</li><li><code>search_for</code> — The pattern to look for</li><li><strong>Returns</strong> — true if the pattern is found in the text, otherwise false</li></ul></details>
</article>
<article class="fn-card" id="does_not_equal" data-text="does_not_equal(value1, value2) checks if two values are different from each other. logic &amp; nulls">
<header><code class="fn-sig">does_not_equal(value1, value2)</code><a class="fn-try" href="https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/#ds=orders&amp;expr=does_not_equal%28%5Bstatus%5D%2C%20%22cancelled%22%29" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Try it ▸</a></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Checks if two values are different from each other.</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>does_not_equal([status], &quot;cancelled&quot;)</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <code class="fn-result">true</code><span class="fn-ctx">when [status] is &quot;shipped&quot;</span></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><code>value1</code> — The column or value to compare</li><li><code>value2</code> — The column or value to compare it against</li><li><strong>Returns</strong> — true if the values are different, false if they are the same</li></ul></details>
</article>
<article class="fn-card" id="equals" data-text="equals(value1, value2) checks if two values are equal to each other. logic &amp; nulls">
<header><code class="fn-sig">equals(value1, value2)</code><a class="fn-try" href="https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/#ds=orders&amp;expr=equals%28%5Bstatus%5D%2C%20%22shipped%22%29" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Try it ▸</a></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Checks if two values are equal to each other.</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>equals([status], &quot;shipped&quot;)</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <code class="fn-result">true</code><span class="fn-ctx">when [status] is &quot;shipped&quot;</span></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><code>value1</code> — The column or value to compare</li><li><code>value2</code> — The column or value to compare it against</li><li><strong>Returns</strong> — true if both values are equal, otherwise false</li></ul></details>
</article>
<article class="fn-card" id="greatest" data-text="greatest(*values) returns the largest value from a list of values. logic &amp; nulls">
<header><code class="fn-sig">greatest(*values)</code><a class="fn-try" href="https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/#ds=orders&amp;expr=greatest%28%5Bprice%5D%2C%20100%29" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Try it ▸</a></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Returns the largest value from a list of values.</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>greatest([price], 100)</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <code class="fn-result">100</code><span class="fn-ctx">when [price] is 79.99</span></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><code>*values</code> — The columns or values to compare</li><li><strong>Returns</strong> — The largest value</li></ul></details>
</article>
<article class="fn-card" id="ifnull" data-text="ifnull(value, default) returns a default value if the input is empty. logic &amp; nulls">
<header><code class="fn-sig">ifnull(value, default)</code><a class="fn-try" href="https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/#ds=orders&amp;expr=ifnull%28%5Bdiscount%5D%2C%200%29" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Try it ▸</a></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Returns a default value if the input is empty.</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>ifnull([discount], 0)</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <code class="fn-result">0</code><span class="fn-ctx">when [discount] is empty</span></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><code>value</code> — The column or value to check</li><li><code>default</code> — The column or value to return if the first value is empty</li><li><strong>Returns</strong> — The original value if not empty, otherwise the default value</li></ul></details>
</article>
<article class="fn-card" id="is_empty" data-text="is_empty(value) checks if a value is empty (missing). logic &amp; nulls">
<header><code class="fn-sig">is_empty(value)</code><a class="fn-try" href="https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/#ds=employees&amp;expr=is_empty%28%5Bemail%5D%29" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Try it ▸</a></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Checks if a value is empty (missing).</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>is_empty([email])</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <code class="fn-result">true</code><span class="fn-ctx">when [email] is empty</span></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><code>value</code> — The column to check</li><li><strong>Returns</strong> — true if the value is empty, otherwise false</li></ul></details>
</article>
<article class="fn-card" id="is_not_empty" data-text="is_not_empty(value) checks if a value contains something (is not missing). logic &amp; nulls">
<header><code class="fn-sig">is_not_empty(value)</code><a class="fn-try" href="https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/#ds=orders&amp;expr=is_not_empty%28%5Bdiscount%5D%29" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Try it ▸</a></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Checks if a value contains something (is not missing).</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>is_not_empty([discount])</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <code class="fn-result">false</code><span class="fn-ctx">when [discount] is empty</span></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><code>value</code> — The column to check</li><li><strong>Returns</strong> — true if the value contains something, false if it is empty</li></ul></details>
</article>
<article class="fn-card" id="is_string" data-text="is_string(value) checks if a literal value is text (a string). this works on fixed values, not on column references. logic &amp; nulls">
<header><code class="fn-sig">is_string(value)</code><a class="fn-try" href="https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/#ds=employees&amp;expr=is_string%28%22hello%22%29" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Try it ▸</a></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Checks if a literal value is text (a string). This works on fixed values, not on column references.</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>is_string(&quot;hello&quot;)</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <code class="fn-result">true</code></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><code>value</code> — The value to check</li><li><strong>Returns</strong> — true if the value is text, otherwise false</li></ul></details>
</article>
<article class="fn-card" id="least" data-text="least(*values) returns the smallest value from a list of values. logic &amp; nulls">
<header><code class="fn-sig">least(*values)</code><a class="fn-try" href="https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/#ds=orders&amp;expr=least%28%5Bprice%5D%2C%20100%29" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Try it ▸</a></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Returns the smallest value from a list of values.</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>least([price], 100)</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <code class="fn-result">100</code><span class="fn-ctx">when [price] is 249.99</span></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><code>*values</code> — The columns or values to compare</li><li><strong>Returns</strong> — The smallest value</li></ul></details>
</article>
<article class="fn-card" id="nullif" data-text="nullif(value1, value2) returns null if the two values are equal, otherwise returns the first value. logic &amp; nulls">
<header><code class="fn-sig">nullif(value1, value2)</code><a class="fn-try" href="https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/#ds=orders&amp;expr=nullif%28%5Bstatus%5D%2C%20%22cancelled%22%29" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Try it ▸</a></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Returns null if the two values are equal, otherwise returns the first value.</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>nullif([status], &quot;cancelled&quot;)</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <code class="fn-result">null</code><span class="fn-ctx">when [status] is &quot;cancelled&quot;</span></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><code>value1</code> — The column or value to return</li><li><code>value2</code> — The value to compare against</li><li><strong>Returns</strong> — null if both are equal, otherwise the first value</li></ul></details>
</article>
<article class="fn-card" id="nvl" data-text="nvl(value, default) returns a default value if the input is empty (alias for ifnull). logic &amp; nulls">
<header><code class="fn-sig">nvl(value, default)</code><a class="fn-try" href="https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/#ds=employees&amp;expr=nvl%28%5Bemail%5D%2C%20%22no%20email%22%29" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Try it ▸</a></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Returns a default value if the input is empty (alias for ifnull).</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>nvl([email], &quot;no email&quot;)</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <code class="fn-result">&quot;no email&quot;</code><span class="fn-ctx">when [email] is empty</span></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><code>value</code> — The column or value to check</li><li><code>default</code> — The column or value to return if the first value is empty</li><li><strong>Returns</strong> — The original value if not empty, otherwise the default value</li></ul></details>
</article>
</div>

<section class="fn-category" id="string" data-cat="string">
<h2>String <span class="fn-count">(23)</span></h2>
<div class="fn-grid">
<article class="fn-card" id="concat" data-text="concat(*text_parts) combines multiple text values into a single text. string">
<header><code class="fn-sig">concat(*text_parts)</code><a class="fn-try" href="https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/#ds=employees&amp;expr=concat%28%5Bfirst_name%5D%2C%20%22%20%22%2C%20%5Blast_name%5D%29" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Try it ▸</a></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Combines multiple text values into a single text.</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>concat([first_name], &quot; &quot;, [last_name])</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <code class="fn-result">&quot;John Doe&quot;</code><span class="fn-ctx">when [first_name] is &quot;John&quot; and [last_name] is &quot;Doe&quot;</span></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><code>*text_parts</code> — The columns or texts you want to combine</li><li><strong>Returns</strong> — The combined text</li></ul></details>
</article>
<article class="fn-card" id="count_match" data-text="count_match(text, pattern) counts how many times a pattern appears in text. string">
<header><code class="fn-sig">count_match(text, pattern)</code><a class="fn-try" href="https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/#ds=employees&amp;expr=count_match%28%5Bemail%5D%2C%20%22.%22%29" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Try it ▸</a></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Counts how many times a pattern appears in text.</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>count_match([email], &quot;.&quot;)</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <code class="fn-result">2</code><span class="fn-ctx">when [email] is &quot;john.doe@company.com&quot;</span></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><code>text</code> — The column or text to search in</li><li><code>pattern</code> — The pattern to search for</li><li><strong>Returns</strong> — The number of matches found</li></ul></details>
</article>
<article class="fn-card" id="ends_with" data-text="ends_with(text, suffix) checks if text ends with a specific suffix. string">
<header><code class="fn-sig">ends_with(text, suffix)</code><a class="fn-try" href="https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/#ds=employees&amp;expr=ends_with%28%5Bemail%5D%2C%20%22.com%22%29" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Try it ▸</a></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Checks if text ends with a specific suffix.</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>ends_with([email], &quot;.com&quot;)</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <code class="fn-result">true</code><span class="fn-ctx">when [email] is &quot;john.doe@company.com&quot;</span></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><code>text</code> — The column or text to check</li><li><code>suffix</code> — The suffix to look for at the end</li><li><strong>Returns</strong> — True if the text ends with the suffix, False otherwise</li></ul></details>
</article>
<article class="fn-card" id="find_position" data-text="find_position(text, sub) finds the position of a substring within text (0-based index). string">
<header><code class="fn-sig">find_position(text, sub)</code><a class="fn-try" href="https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/#ds=orders&amp;expr=find_position%28%5Border_id%5D%2C%20%22-%22%29" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Try it ▸</a></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Finds the position of a substring within text (0-based index).</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>find_position([order_id], &quot;-&quot;)</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <code class="fn-result">3</code><span class="fn-ctx">when [order_id] is &quot;ORD-0001&quot;</span></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><code>text</code> — The column or text to search in</li><li><code>sub</code> — The substring to find the position of</li><li><strong>Returns</strong> — The position of the substring, or null if it is not found</li></ul></details>
</article>
<article class="fn-card" id="left" data-text="left(text, num_chars) gets a specified number of characters from the beginning of text. string">
<header><code class="fn-sig">left(text, num_chars)</code><a class="fn-try" href="https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/#ds=orders&amp;expr=left%28%5Border_id%5D%2C%203%29" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Try it ▸</a></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Gets a specified number of characters from the beginning of text.</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>left([order_id], 3)</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <code class="fn-result">&quot;ORD&quot;</code><span class="fn-ctx">when [order_id] is &quot;ORD-0001&quot;</span></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><code>text</code> — The column or text to extract from</li><li><code>num_chars</code> — How many characters to take from the beginning</li><li><strong>Returns</strong> — The extracted text</li></ul></details>
</article>
<article class="fn-card" id="left_trim" data-text="left_trim(text) removes spaces from the beginning of text. string">
<header><code class="fn-sig">left_trim(text)</code><a class="fn-try" href="https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/#ds=events&amp;expr=left_trim%28%5Bcity%5D%29" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Try it ▸</a></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Removes spaces from the beginning of text.</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>left_trim([city])</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <code class="fn-result">&quot;Amsterdam &quot;</code><span class="fn-ctx">when [city] is &quot; Amsterdam &quot;</span></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><code>text</code> — The column or text you want to trim</li><li><strong>Returns</strong> — The trimmed text</li></ul></details>
</article>
<article class="fn-card" id="length" data-text="length(text) counts the number of characters in text. string">
<header><code class="fn-sig">length(text)</code><a class="fn-try" href="https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/#ds=events&amp;expr=length%28%5Bcity%5D%29" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Try it ▸</a></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Counts the number of characters in text.</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>length([city])</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <code class="fn-result">9</code><span class="fn-ctx">when [city] is &quot;Amsterdam&quot;</span></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><code>text</code> — The column or text to measure</li><li><strong>Returns</strong> — The number of characters</li></ul></details>
</article>
<article class="fn-card" id="lowercase" data-text="lowercase(text) converts text to all lowercase. string">
<header><code class="fn-sig">lowercase(text)</code><a class="fn-try" href="https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/#ds=employees&amp;expr=lowercase%28%5Bdepartment%5D%29" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Try it ▸</a></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Converts text to all lowercase.</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>lowercase([department])</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <code class="fn-result">&quot;engineering&quot;</code><span class="fn-ctx">when [department] is &quot;Engineering&quot;</span></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><code>text</code> — The column or text to convert</li><li><strong>Returns</strong> — The lowercase text</li></ul></details>
</article>
<article class="fn-card" id="mid" data-text="mid(text, start, num_chars) extracts a portion of text from the middle starting at a specified position. string">
<header><code class="fn-sig">mid(text, start, num_chars)</code><a class="fn-try" href="https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/#ds=orders&amp;expr=mid%28%5Border_id%5D%2C%204%2C%204%29" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Try it ▸</a></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Extracts a portion of text from the middle starting at a specified position.</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>mid([order_id], 4, 4)</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <code class="fn-result">&quot;0001&quot;</code><span class="fn-ctx">when [order_id] is &quot;ORD-0001&quot;</span></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><code>text</code> — The column or text to extract from</li><li><code>start</code> — The starting position (0-based index)</li><li><code>num_chars</code> — How many characters to extract</li><li><strong>Returns</strong> — The extracted text</li></ul></details>
</article>
<article class="fn-card" id="pad_left" data-text="pad_left(text, length, pad_character=&#x27; &#x27;) adds characters to the beginning of text to reach a specific length. string">
<header><code class="fn-sig">pad_left(text, length, pad_character=&#x27; &#x27;)</code><a class="fn-try" href="https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/#ds=orders&amp;expr=pad_left%28%5Border_id%5D%2C%2012%2C%20%220%22%29" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Try it ▸</a></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Adds characters to the beginning of text to reach a specific length.</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>pad_left([order_id], 12, &quot;0&quot;)</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <code class="fn-result">&quot;0000ORD-0001&quot;</code><span class="fn-ctx">when [order_id] is &quot;ORD-0001&quot;</span></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><code>text</code> — The column or text you want to pad</li><li><code>length</code> — How long you want the final text to be</li><li><code>pad_character</code> — What character to use for padding (default is a space)</li><li><strong>Returns</strong> — The padded text</li></ul></details>
</article>
<article class="fn-card" id="pad_right" data-text="pad_right(text, length, pad_character=&#x27; &#x27;) adds characters to the end of text to reach a specific length. string">
<header><code class="fn-sig">pad_right(text, length, pad_character=&#x27; &#x27;)</code><a class="fn-try" href="https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/#ds=orders&amp;expr=pad_right%28%5Border_id%5D%2C%2012%2C%20%220%22%29" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Try it ▸</a></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Adds characters to the end of text to reach a specific length.</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>pad_right([order_id], 12, &quot;0&quot;)</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <code class="fn-result">&quot;ORD-00010000&quot;</code><span class="fn-ctx">when [order_id] is &quot;ORD-0001&quot;</span></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><code>text</code> — The column or text you want to pad</li><li><code>length</code> — How long you want the final text to be</li><li><code>pad_character</code> — What character to use for padding (default is a space)</li><li><strong>Returns</strong> — The padded text</li></ul></details>
</article>
<article class="fn-card" id="repeat" data-text="repeat(text, count) repeats text a specified number of times. string">
<header><code class="fn-sig">repeat(text, count)</code><a class="fn-try" href="https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/#ds=employees&amp;expr=repeat%28%5Bfirst_name%5D%2C%202%29" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Try it ▸</a></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Repeats text a specified number of times.</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>repeat([first_name], 2)</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <code class="fn-result">&quot;JohnJohn&quot;</code><span class="fn-ctx">when [first_name] is &quot;John&quot;</span></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><code>text</code> — The column or text to repeat</li><li><code>count</code> — How many times to repeat the text</li><li><strong>Returns</strong> — The repeated text</li></ul></details>
</article>
<article class="fn-card" id="replace" data-text="replace(text, find_text, replace_with) replaces specific text with different text. string">
<header><code class="fn-sig">replace(text, find_text, replace_with)</code><a class="fn-try" href="https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/#ds=employees&amp;expr=replace%28%5Bemail%5D%2C%20%22.com%22%2C%20%22.org%22%29" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Try it ▸</a></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Replaces specific text with different text.</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>replace([email], &quot;.com&quot;, &quot;.org&quot;)</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <code class="fn-result">&quot;john.doe@company.org&quot;</code><span class="fn-ctx">when [email] is &quot;john.doe@company.com&quot;</span></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><code>text</code> — The column or text where replacements will be made</li><li><code>find_text</code> — The text you want to find and replace</li><li><code>replace_with</code> — The new text that will replace the found text</li><li><strong>Returns</strong> — The text after replacement</li></ul></details>
</article>
<article class="fn-card" id="reverse" data-text="reverse(text) reverses the characters in text. string">
<header><code class="fn-sig">reverse(text)</code><a class="fn-try" href="https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/#ds=employees&amp;expr=reverse%28%5Bfirst_name%5D%29" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Try it ▸</a></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Reverses the characters in text.</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>reverse([first_name])</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <code class="fn-result">&quot;nhoJ&quot;</code><span class="fn-ctx">when [first_name] is &quot;John&quot;</span></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><code>text</code> — The column or text to reverse</li><li><strong>Returns</strong> — The reversed text</li></ul></details>
</article>
<article class="fn-card" id="right" data-text="right(text, num_chars) gets a specified number of characters from the end of text. string">
<header><code class="fn-sig">right(text, num_chars)</code><a class="fn-try" href="https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/#ds=orders&amp;expr=right%28%5Border_id%5D%2C%204%29" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Try it ▸</a></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Gets a specified number of characters from the end of text.</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>right([order_id], 4)</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <code class="fn-result">&quot;0001&quot;</code><span class="fn-ctx">when [order_id] is &quot;ORD-0001&quot;</span></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><code>text</code> — The column or text to extract from</li><li><code>num_chars</code> — How many characters to take from the end</li><li><strong>Returns</strong> — The extracted text</li></ul></details>
</article>
<article class="fn-card" id="right_trim" data-text="right_trim(text) removes spaces from the end of text. string">
<header><code class="fn-sig">right_trim(text)</code><a class="fn-try" href="https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/#ds=events&amp;expr=right_trim%28%5Bcity%5D%29" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Try it ▸</a></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Removes spaces from the end of text.</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>right_trim([city])</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <code class="fn-result">&quot;Amsterdam&quot;</code><span class="fn-ctx">when [city] is &quot;Amsterdam &quot;</span></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><code>text</code> — The column or text you want to trim</li><li><strong>Returns</strong> — The trimmed text</li></ul></details>
</article>
<article class="fn-card" id="split" data-text="split(text, delimiter) splits text into a list using a delimiter. string">
<header><code class="fn-sig">split(text, delimiter)</code><a class="fn-try" href="https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/#ds=orders&amp;expr=split%28%5Bproduct%5D%2C%20%22%20%22%29" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Try it ▸</a></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Splits text into a list using a delimiter.</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>split([product], &quot; &quot;)</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <span class="fn-result">[&quot;Laptop&quot;, &quot;Pro&quot;]</span><span class="fn-ctx">when [product] is &quot;Laptop Pro&quot;</span></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><code>text</code> — The column or text to split</li><li><code>delimiter</code> — The character(s) to split on</li><li><strong>Returns</strong> — A list of text parts</li></ul></details>
</article>
<article class="fn-card" id="starts_with" data-text="starts_with(text, prefix) checks if text starts with a specific prefix. string">
<header><code class="fn-sig">starts_with(text, prefix)</code><a class="fn-try" href="https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/#ds=orders&amp;expr=starts_with%28%5Border_id%5D%2C%20%22ORD%22%29" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Try it ▸</a></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Checks if text starts with a specific prefix.</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>starts_with([order_id], &quot;ORD&quot;)</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <code class="fn-result">true</code><span class="fn-ctx">when [order_id] is &quot;ORD-0001&quot;</span></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><code>text</code> — The column or text to check</li><li><code>prefix</code> — The prefix to look for at the start</li><li><strong>Returns</strong> — True if the text starts with the prefix, False otherwise</li></ul></details>
</article>
<article class="fn-card" id="string_similarity" data-text="string_similarity(text1, text2, method=&#x27;levenshtein&#x27;) measures how similar two texts are to each other, on a scale of 0 to 1. a value of 1 means the texts are identical, while 0 means they are completely different. string">
<header><code class="fn-sig">string_similarity(text1, text2, method=&#x27;levenshtein&#x27;)</code></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Measures how similar two texts are to each other, on a scale of 0 to 1. A value of 1 means the texts are identical, while 0 means they are completely different.</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>string_similarity([first_name], [last_name], &quot;levenshtein&quot;)</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <code class="fn-result">0.4</code><span class="fn-ctx">when [first_name] is &quot;John&quot; and [last_name] is &quot;Jones&quot;</span></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><code>text1</code> — The first column or text to compare</li><li><code>text2</code> — The second column or text to compare</li><li><code>method</code> — Which comparison method to use (default is &#x27;levenshtein&#x27;)</li><li><strong>Returns</strong> — A similarity score between 0 and 1</li></ul></details>
</article>
<article class="fn-card" id="substring" data-text="substring(text, start, num_chars) extracts a portion of text starting at a specified position (alias for mid). string">
<header><code class="fn-sig">substring(text, start, num_chars)</code><a class="fn-try" href="https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/#ds=orders&amp;expr=substring%28%5Border_id%5D%2C%200%2C%203%29" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Try it ▸</a></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Extracts a portion of text starting at a specified position (alias for mid).</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>substring([order_id], 0, 3)</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <code class="fn-result">&quot;ORD&quot;</code><span class="fn-ctx">when [order_id] is &quot;ORD-0001&quot;</span></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><code>text</code> — The column or text to extract from</li><li><code>start</code> — The starting position (0-based index)</li><li><code>num_chars</code> — How many characters to extract</li><li><strong>Returns</strong> — The extracted text</li></ul></details>
</article>
<article class="fn-card" id="titlecase" data-text="titlecase(text) converts text to title case, where each word is capitalized. string">
<header><code class="fn-sig">titlecase(text)</code><a class="fn-try" href="https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/#ds=events&amp;expr=titlecase%28%5Bevent%5D%29" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Try it ▸</a></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Converts Text To Title Case, Where Each Word Is Capitalized.</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>titlecase([event])</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <code class="fn-result">&quot;Tech Conference&quot;</code><span class="fn-ctx">when [event] is &quot;tech conference&quot;</span></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><code>text</code> — The column or text to convert</li><li><strong>Returns</strong> — The title case text</li></ul></details>
</article>
<article class="fn-card" id="trim" data-text="trim(text) removes spaces from both the beginning and end of text. string">
<header><code class="fn-sig">trim(text)</code><a class="fn-try" href="https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/#ds=events&amp;expr=trim%28%5Bcity%5D%29" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Try it ▸</a></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Removes spaces from both the beginning and end of text.</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>trim([city])</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <code class="fn-result">&quot;Amsterdam&quot;</code><span class="fn-ctx">when [city] is &quot; Amsterdam &quot;</span></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><code>text</code> — The column or text you want to trim</li><li><strong>Returns</strong> — The trimmed text</li></ul></details>
</article>
<article class="fn-card" id="uppercase" data-text="uppercase(text) converts text to all uppercase. string">
<header><code class="fn-sig">uppercase(text)</code><a class="fn-try" href="https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/#ds=events&amp;expr=uppercase%28%5Bcity%5D%29" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Try it ▸</a></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Converts text to ALL UPPERCASE.</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>uppercase([city])</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <code class="fn-result">&quot;AMSTERDAM&quot;</code><span class="fn-ctx">when [city] is &quot;Amsterdam&quot;</span></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><code>text</code> — The column or text to convert</li><li><strong>Returns</strong> — The uppercase text</li></ul></details>
</article>
</div>
</section>

<section class="fn-category" id="math" data-cat="math">
<h2>Math <span class="fn-count">(23)</span></h2>
<div class="fn-grid">
<article class="fn-card" id="abs" data-text="abs(number) returns the absolute value of each value in a column (removes the negative sign). math">
<header><code class="fn-sig">abs(number)</code><a class="fn-try" href="https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/#ds=orders&amp;expr=abs%28%5Bprice%5D%29" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Try it ▸</a></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Returns the absolute value of each value in a column (removes the negative sign).</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>abs([price])</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <code class="fn-result">5.0</code><span class="fn-ctx">when [price] is -5.0</span></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><code>number</code> — The column or value to get the absolute value of</li><li><strong>Returns</strong> — The non-negative version of the value</li></ul></details>
</article>
<article class="fn-card" id="acos" data-text="acos(number) calculates the arccosine (inverse cosine) of each value in a column. math">
<header><code class="fn-sig">acos(number)</code><a class="fn-try" href="https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/#ds=orders&amp;expr=acos%28%5Bdiscount%5D%29" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Try it ▸</a></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Calculates the arccosine (inverse cosine) of each value in a column.</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>acos([ratio])</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <code class="fn-result">0.0</code><span class="fn-ctx">when [ratio] is 1</span></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><code>number</code> — The column or value to take the arccosine of (values between -1 and 1)</li><li><strong>Returns</strong> — The angle in radians whose cosine equals the value</li></ul></details>
</article>
<article class="fn-card" id="asin" data-text="asin(number) calculates the arcsine (inverse sine) of each value in a column. math">
<header><code class="fn-sig">asin(number)</code><a class="fn-try" href="https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/#ds=orders&amp;expr=asin%28%5Bdiscount%5D%29" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Try it ▸</a></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Calculates the arcsine (inverse sine) of each value in a column.</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>asin([ratio])</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <code class="fn-result">0.0</code><span class="fn-ctx">when [ratio] is 0</span></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><code>number</code> — The column or value to take the arcsine of (values between -1 and 1)</li><li><strong>Returns</strong> — The angle in radians whose sine equals the value</li></ul></details>
</article>
<article class="fn-card" id="atan" data-text="atan(number) calculates the arctangent (inverse tangent) of each value in a column. math">
<header><code class="fn-sig">atan(number)</code><a class="fn-try" href="https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/#ds=orders&amp;expr=atan%28%5Bdiscount%5D%29" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Try it ▸</a></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Calculates the arctangent (inverse tangent) of each value in a column.</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>atan([ratio])</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <code class="fn-result">0.0</code><span class="fn-ctx">when [ratio] is 0</span></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><code>number</code> — The column or value to take the arctangent of</li><li><strong>Returns</strong> — The angle in radians whose tangent equals the value</li></ul></details>
</article>
<article class="fn-card" id="ceil" data-text="ceil(number) rounds each value in a column up to the nearest whole number. math">
<header><code class="fn-sig">ceil(number)</code><a class="fn-try" href="https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/#ds=orders&amp;expr=ceil%28%5Bprice%5D%29" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Try it ▸</a></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Rounds each value in a column up to the nearest whole number.</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>ceil([price])</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <code class="fn-result">5.0</code><span class="fn-ctx">when [price] is 4.2</span></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><code>number</code> — The column or value to round up</li><li><strong>Returns</strong> — The value rounded up to a whole number</li></ul></details>
</article>
<article class="fn-card" id="cos" data-text="cos(angle) calculates the cosine of each value in a column, where values are angles in radians. math">
<header><code class="fn-sig">cos(angle)</code><a class="fn-try" href="https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/#ds=orders&amp;expr=cos%28%5Bdiscount%5D%29" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Try it ▸</a></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Calculates the cosine of each value in a column, where values are angles in radians.</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>cos([angle])</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <code class="fn-result">1.0</code><span class="fn-ctx">when [angle] is 0</span></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><code>angle</code> — The column or value with angles in radians</li><li><strong>Returns</strong> — The cosine of the angle</li></ul></details>
</article>
<article class="fn-card" id="exp" data-text="exp(number) calculates e raised to the power of each value in a column. math">
<header><code class="fn-sig">exp(number)</code><a class="fn-try" href="https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/#ds=orders&amp;expr=exp%28%5Bdiscount%5D%29" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Try it ▸</a></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Calculates e raised to the power of each value in a column.</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>exp([discount])</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <code class="fn-result">1.0</code><span class="fn-ctx">when [discount] is 0.0</span></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><code>number</code> — The column or value to use as the exponent of e</li><li><strong>Returns</strong> — The result of e raised to the value</li></ul></details>
</article>
<article class="fn-card" id="floor" data-text="floor(number) rounds each value in a column down to the nearest whole number. math">
<header><code class="fn-sig">floor(number)</code><a class="fn-try" href="https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/#ds=orders&amp;expr=floor%28%5Bprice%5D%29" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Try it ▸</a></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Rounds each value in a column down to the nearest whole number.</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>floor([price])</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <code class="fn-result">4.0</code><span class="fn-ctx">when [price] is 4.7</span></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><code>number</code> — The column or value to round down</li><li><strong>Returns</strong> — The value rounded down to a whole number</li></ul></details>
</article>
<article class="fn-card" id="log" data-text="log(number) calculates the natural logarithm (base e) of each value in a column. math">
<header><code class="fn-sig">log(number)</code><a class="fn-try" href="https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/#ds=orders&amp;expr=log%28%5Bprice%5D%29" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Try it ▸</a></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Calculates the natural logarithm (base e) of each value in a column.</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>log([price])</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <code class="fn-result">0.0</code><span class="fn-ctx">when [price] is 1.0</span></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><code>number</code> — The column or value to take the natural logarithm of</li><li><strong>Returns</strong> — The natural logarithm of the value</li></ul></details>
</article>
<article class="fn-card" id="log10" data-text="log10(number) calculates the base-10 logarithm of each value in a column. math">
<header><code class="fn-sig">log10(number)</code><a class="fn-try" href="https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/#ds=employees&amp;expr=log10%28%5Bsalary%5D%29" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Try it ▸</a></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Calculates the base-10 logarithm of each value in a column.</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>log10([salary])</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <code class="fn-result">4.0</code><span class="fn-ctx">when [salary] is 10000.0</span></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><code>number</code> — The column or value to take the base-10 logarithm of</li><li><strong>Returns</strong> — The base-10 logarithm of the value</li></ul></details>
</article>
<article class="fn-card" id="log2" data-text="log2(number) calculates the base-2 logarithm of each value in a column. math">
<header><code class="fn-sig">log2(number)</code><a class="fn-try" href="https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/#ds=orders&amp;expr=log2%28%5Bprice%5D%29" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Try it ▸</a></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Calculates the base-2 logarithm of each value in a column.</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>log2([price])</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <code class="fn-result">3.0</code><span class="fn-ctx">when [price] is 8.0</span></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><code>number</code> — The column or value to take the base-2 logarithm of</li><li><strong>Returns</strong> — The base-2 logarithm of the value</li></ul></details>
</article>
<article class="fn-card" id="mod" data-text="mod(dividend, divisor) calculates the remainder after dividing each value in a column (modulo). math">
<header><code class="fn-sig">mod(dividend, divisor)</code><a class="fn-try" href="https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/#ds=orders&amp;expr=mod%28%5Bquantity%5D%2C%203%29" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Try it ▸</a></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Calculates the remainder after dividing each value in a column (modulo).</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>mod([quantity], 3)</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <code class="fn-result">1</code><span class="fn-ctx">when [quantity] is 10</span></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><code>dividend</code> — The column or value to be divided</li><li><code>divisor</code> — The number to divide by</li><li><strong>Returns</strong> — The remainder of the division</li></ul></details>
</article>
<article class="fn-card" id="negation" data-text="negation(number) flips the sign of each value in a column (positive becomes negative, negative becomes positive). math">
<header><code class="fn-sig">negation(number)</code></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Flips the sign of each value in a column (positive becomes negative, negative becomes positive).</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>negation([price])</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <code class="fn-result">-19.99</code><span class="fn-ctx">when [price] is 19.99</span></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><code>number</code> — The column or value to flip the sign of</li><li><strong>Returns</strong> — The value with its sign flipped</li></ul></details>
</article>
<article class="fn-card" id="negative" data-text="negative() returns the constant value -1; it takes no arguments. math">
<header><code class="fn-sig">negative()</code><a class="fn-try" href="https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/#ds=employees&amp;expr=negative%28%29" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Try it ▸</a></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Returns the constant value -1; it takes no arguments.</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>negative()</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <code class="fn-result">-1</code></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><strong>Returns</strong> — The value -1</li></ul></details>
</article>
<article class="fn-card" id="pow" data-text="pow(base, exponent) raises each value in a column to a power (alias for power). math">
<header><code class="fn-sig">pow(base, exponent)</code><a class="fn-try" href="https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/#ds=orders&amp;expr=pow%28%5Bquantity%5D%2C%202%29" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Try it ▸</a></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Raises each value in a column to a power (alias for power).</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>pow([quantity], 2)</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <code class="fn-result">16</code><span class="fn-ctx">when [quantity] is 4</span></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><code>base</code> — The column or value to raise</li><li><code>exponent</code> — The power to raise the base to</li><li><strong>Returns</strong> — The result of base raised to the power of exponent</li></ul></details>
</article>
<article class="fn-card" id="power" data-text="power(base, exponent) raises each value in a column to a power. math">
<header><code class="fn-sig">power(base, exponent)</code><a class="fn-try" href="https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/#ds=orders&amp;expr=power%28%5Bquantity%5D%2C%202%29" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Try it ▸</a></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Raises each value in a column to a power.</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>power([quantity], 2)</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <code class="fn-result">9</code><span class="fn-ctx">when [quantity] is 3</span></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><code>base</code> — The column or value to raise</li><li><code>exponent</code> — The power to raise the base to</li><li><strong>Returns</strong> — The result of base raised to the power of exponent</li></ul></details>
</article>
<article class="fn-card" id="random_int" data-text="random_int(min_value=0, max_value=2) generates a random whole number for each row, from min_value (inclusive) up to max_value (exclusive). math">
<header><code class="fn-sig">random_int(min_value=0, max_value=2)</code><a class="fn-try" href="https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/#ds=employees&amp;expr=random_int%281%2C%20100%29" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Try it ▸</a></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Generates a random whole number for each row, from min_value (inclusive) up to max_value (exclusive).</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>random_int(1, 100)</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <span class="fn-result">a random value such as 57</span></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><code>min_value</code> — The smallest possible number to generate (default is 0)</li><li><code>max_value</code> — The upper bound, which is never reached (default is 2)</li><li><strong>Returns</strong> — A random whole number for each row in the given range</li></ul></details>
</article>
<article class="fn-card" id="round" data-text="round(number, decimal_places=none) rounds each value in a column to a specified number of decimal places. math">
<header><code class="fn-sig">round(number, decimal_places=None)</code><a class="fn-try" href="https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/#ds=orders&amp;expr=round%28%5Bprice%5D%2C%202%29" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Try it ▸</a></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Rounds each value in a column to a specified number of decimal places.</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>round([price], 2)</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <code class="fn-result">19.99</code><span class="fn-ctx">when [price] is 19.987</span></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><code>number</code> — The column or value to round</li><li><code>decimal_places</code> — How many decimal places to keep (default is 0)</li><li><strong>Returns</strong> — The rounded value</li></ul></details>
</article>
<article class="fn-card" id="sign" data-text="sign(number) returns the sign of each value in a column (-1, 0, or 1). math">
<header><code class="fn-sig">sign(number)</code><a class="fn-try" href="https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/#ds=orders&amp;expr=sign%28%5Bquantity%5D%29" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Try it ▸</a></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Returns the sign of each value in a column (-1, 0, or 1).</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>sign([quantity])</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <code class="fn-result">-1</code><span class="fn-ctx">when [quantity] is -2</span></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><code>number</code> — The column or value to check</li><li><strong>Returns</strong> — 1 if negative, 0 if zero, 1 if positive</li></ul></details>
</article>
<article class="fn-card" id="sin" data-text="sin(angle) calculates the sine of each value in a column, where values are angles in radians. math">
<header><code class="fn-sig">sin(angle)</code><a class="fn-try" href="https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/#ds=orders&amp;expr=sin%28%5Bdiscount%5D%29" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Try it ▸</a></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Calculates the sine of each value in a column, where values are angles in radians.</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>sin([angle])</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <code class="fn-result">0.0</code><span class="fn-ctx">when [angle] is 0</span></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><code>angle</code> — The column or value with angles in radians</li><li><strong>Returns</strong> — The sine of the angle</li></ul></details>
</article>
<article class="fn-card" id="sqrt" data-text="sqrt(number) calculates the square root of each value in a column. math">
<header><code class="fn-sig">sqrt(number)</code><a class="fn-try" href="https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/#ds=orders&amp;expr=sqrt%28%5Bprice%5D%29" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Try it ▸</a></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Calculates the square root of each value in a column.</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>sqrt([price])</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <code class="fn-result">3.0</code><span class="fn-ctx">when [price] is 9.0</span></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><code>number</code> — The column or value to take the square root of</li><li><strong>Returns</strong> — The square root of the value</li></ul></details>
</article>
<article class="fn-card" id="tan" data-text="tan(angle) calculates the tangent of each value in a column, where values are angles in radians. math">
<header><code class="fn-sig">tan(angle)</code><a class="fn-try" href="https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/#ds=orders&amp;expr=tan%28%5Bdiscount%5D%29" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Try it ▸</a></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Calculates the tangent of each value in a column, where values are angles in radians.</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>tan([angle])</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <code class="fn-result">0.0</code><span class="fn-ctx">when [angle] is 0</span></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><code>angle</code> — The column or value with angles in radians</li><li><strong>Returns</strong> — The tangent of the angle</li></ul></details>
</article>
<article class="fn-card" id="tanh" data-text="tanh(number) calculates the hyperbolic tangent of each value in a column; the result is always between -1 and 1. math">
<header><code class="fn-sig">tanh(number)</code><a class="fn-try" href="https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/#ds=orders&amp;expr=tanh%28%5Bdiscount%5D%29" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Try it ▸</a></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Calculates the hyperbolic tangent of each value in a column; the result is always between -1 and 1.</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>tanh([discount])</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <code class="fn-result">0.0</code><span class="fn-ctx">when [discount] is 0.0</span></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><code>number</code> — The column or value to take the hyperbolic tangent of</li><li><strong>Returns</strong> — The hyperbolic tangent of the value</li></ul></details>
</article>
</div>
</section>

<section class="fn-category" id="date-time" data-cat="date-time">
<h2>Date &amp; Time <span class="fn-count">(28)</span></h2>
<div class="fn-grid">
<article class="fn-card" id="add_days" data-text="add_days(date_value, days) adds a number of days to a date. date &amp; time">
<header><code class="fn-sig">add_days(date_value, days)</code><a class="fn-try" href="https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/#ds=employees&amp;expr=add_days%28%5Bhire_date%5D%2C%205%29" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Try it ▸</a></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Adds a number of days to a date.</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>add_days([hire_date], 5)</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <code class="fn-result">2021-03-20</code><span class="fn-ctx">when [hire_date] is 2021-03-15</span></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><code>date_value</code> — The date column or expression to add days to</li><li><code>days</code> — How many days to add</li><li><strong>Returns</strong> — The new date</li></ul></details>
</article>
<article class="fn-card" id="add_hours" data-text="add_hours(date_value, hours) adds a number of hours to a datetime. date &amp; time">
<header><code class="fn-sig">add_hours(date_value, hours)</code><a class="fn-try" href="https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/#ds=orders&amp;expr=add_hours%28%5Border_date%5D%2C%203%29" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Try it ▸</a></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Adds a number of hours to a datetime.</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>add_hours([order_date], 3)</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <span class="fn-result">2024-01-15 13:30:00</span><span class="fn-ctx">when [order_date] is 2024-01-15 10:30:00</span></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><code>date_value</code> — The datetime column or expression to add hours to</li><li><code>hours</code> — How many hours to add</li><li><strong>Returns</strong> — The new date and time</li></ul></details>
</article>
<article class="fn-card" id="add_minutes" data-text="add_minutes(date_value, minutes) adds a number of minutes to a datetime. date &amp; time">
<header><code class="fn-sig">add_minutes(date_value, minutes)</code><a class="fn-try" href="https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/#ds=orders&amp;expr=add_minutes%28%5Border_date%5D%2C%2015%29" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Try it ▸</a></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Adds a number of minutes to a datetime.</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>add_minutes([order_date], 15)</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <span class="fn-result">2024-01-15 10:45:00</span><span class="fn-ctx">when [order_date] is 2024-01-15 10:30:00</span></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><code>date_value</code> — The datetime column or expression to add minutes to</li><li><code>minutes</code> — How many minutes to add</li><li><strong>Returns</strong> — The new date and time</li></ul></details>
</article>
<article class="fn-card" id="add_months" data-text="add_months(date_value, months) adds a number of months to a date. date &amp; time">
<header><code class="fn-sig">add_months(date_value, months)</code><a class="fn-try" href="https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/#ds=employees&amp;expr=add_months%28%5Bhire_date%5D%2C%202%29" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Try it ▸</a></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Adds a number of months to a date.</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>add_months([hire_date], 2)</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <code class="fn-result">2021-05-15</code><span class="fn-ctx">when [hire_date] is 2021-03-15</span></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><code>date_value</code> — The date column or expression to add months to</li><li><code>months</code> — How many months to add</li><li><strong>Returns</strong> — The new date</li></ul></details>
</article>
<article class="fn-card" id="add_seconds" data-text="add_seconds(date_value, seconds) adds a number of seconds to a datetime. date &amp; time">
<header><code class="fn-sig">add_seconds(date_value, seconds)</code><a class="fn-try" href="https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/#ds=orders&amp;expr=add_seconds%28%5Border_date%5D%2C%2030%29" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Try it ▸</a></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Adds a number of seconds to a datetime.</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>add_seconds([order_date], 30)</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <span class="fn-result">2024-01-15 10:30:30</span><span class="fn-ctx">when [order_date] is 2024-01-15 10:30:00</span></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><code>date_value</code> — The datetime column or expression to add seconds to</li><li><code>seconds</code> — How many seconds to add</li><li><strong>Returns</strong> — The new date and time</li></ul></details>
</article>
<article class="fn-card" id="add_weeks" data-text="add_weeks(date_value, weeks) adds a number of weeks to a date. date &amp; time">
<header><code class="fn-sig">add_weeks(date_value, weeks)</code><a class="fn-try" href="https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/#ds=employees&amp;expr=add_weeks%28%5Bhire_date%5D%2C%202%29" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Try it ▸</a></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Adds a number of weeks to a date.</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>add_weeks([hire_date], 2)</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <code class="fn-result">2021-03-29</code><span class="fn-ctx">when [hire_date] is 2021-03-15</span></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><code>date_value</code> — The date column or expression to add weeks to</li><li><code>weeks</code> — How many weeks to add</li><li><strong>Returns</strong> — The new date</li></ul></details>
</article>
<article class="fn-card" id="add_years" data-text="add_years(date_value, years) adds a number of years to a date. date &amp; time">
<header><code class="fn-sig">add_years(date_value, years)</code><a class="fn-try" href="https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/#ds=employees&amp;expr=add_years%28%5Bhire_date%5D%2C%201%29" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Try it ▸</a></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Adds a number of years to a date.</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>add_years([hire_date], 1)</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <code class="fn-result">2022-03-15</code><span class="fn-ctx">when [hire_date] is 2021-03-15</span></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><code>date_value</code> — The date column or expression to add years to</li><li><code>years</code> — How many years to add</li><li><strong>Returns</strong> — The new date</li></ul></details>
</article>
<article class="fn-card" id="date_diff_days" data-text="date_diff_days(date1, date2) calculates the number of days between two dates (the first minus the second). date &amp; time">
<header><code class="fn-sig">date_diff_days(date1, date2)</code><a class="fn-try" href="https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/#ds=employees&amp;expr=date_diff_days%28today%28%29%2C%20%5Bhire_date%5D%29" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Try it ▸</a></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Calculates the number of days between two dates (the first minus the second).</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>date_diff_days(today(), [hire_date])</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <code class="fn-result">30</code><span class="fn-ctx">when [hire_date] is 30 days before today</span></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><code>date1</code> — The date column or expression to subtract from</li><li><code>date2</code> — The date column or expression to subtract</li><li><strong>Returns</strong> — The number of days between the two dates</li></ul></details>
</article>
<article class="fn-card" id="date_trim" data-text="date_trim(date_value, part) removes the smaller parts of a date or time. date &amp; time">
<header><code class="fn-sig">date_trim(date_value, part)</code><a class="fn-try" href="https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/#ds=orders&amp;expr=date_trim%28%5Border_date%5D%2C%20%22day%22%29" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Try it ▸</a></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Removes the smaller parts of a date or time.</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>date_trim([order_date], &quot;day&quot;)</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <span class="fn-result">2024-01-15 00:00:00</span><span class="fn-ctx">when [order_date] is 2024-01-15 10:30:45</span></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><code>date_value</code> — The datetime column or expression to trim</li><li><code>part</code> — Which part to keep (&#x27;year&#x27;, &#x27;month&#x27;, &#x27;day&#x27;, &#x27;hour&#x27;, &#x27;minute&#x27;, or &#x27;second&#x27;)</li><li><strong>Returns</strong> — The trimmed date and time</li></ul></details>
</article>
<article class="fn-card" id="date_truncate" data-text="date_truncate(date_value, truncate_by) rounds a date down to the nearest specified unit. date &amp; time">
<header><code class="fn-sig">date_truncate(date_value, truncate_by)</code><a class="fn-try" href="https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/#ds=orders&amp;expr=date_truncate%28%5Border_date%5D%2C%20%221d%22%29" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Try it ▸</a></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Rounds a date down to the nearest specified unit.</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>date_truncate([order_date], &quot;1d&quot;)</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <span class="fn-result">2024-01-15 00:00:00</span><span class="fn-ctx">when [order_date] is 2024-01-15 10:30:45</span></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><code>date_value</code> — The datetime column or expression to truncate</li><li><code>truncate_by</code> — The time unit to round down to (like &quot;1d&quot;, &quot;2h&quot;, &quot;15m&quot;)</li><li><strong>Returns</strong> — The truncated date and time</li></ul></details>
</article>
<article class="fn-card" id="datetime_diff_nanoseconds" data-text="datetime_diff_nanoseconds(date1, date2) calculates the number of nanoseconds between two datetimes (the first minus the second), for very precise time measurements. date &amp; time">
<header><code class="fn-sig">datetime_diff_nanoseconds(date1, date2)</code><a class="fn-try" href="https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/#ds=events&amp;expr=datetime_diff_nanoseconds%28%5Bend%5D%2C%20%5Bstart%5D%29" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Try it ▸</a></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Calculates the number of nanoseconds between two datetimes (the first minus the second), for very precise time measurements.</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>datetime_diff_nanoseconds([end], [start])</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <code class="fn-result">1000000000</code><span class="fn-ctx">when [start] is 2024-01-15 10:00:00 and [end] is 2024-01-15 10:00:01</span></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><code>date1</code> — The datetime column or expression to subtract from</li><li><code>date2</code> — The datetime column or expression to subtract</li><li><strong>Returns</strong> — The number of nanoseconds between the two datetimes</li></ul></details>
</article>
<article class="fn-card" id="datetime_diff_seconds" data-text="datetime_diff_seconds(date1, date2) calculates the number of seconds between two datetimes (the first minus the second). date &amp; time">
<header><code class="fn-sig">datetime_diff_seconds(date1, date2)</code><a class="fn-try" href="https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/#ds=events&amp;expr=datetime_diff_seconds%28%5Bend%5D%2C%20%5Bstart%5D%29" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Try it ▸</a></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Calculates the number of seconds between two datetimes (the first minus the second).</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>datetime_diff_seconds([end], [start])</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <code class="fn-result">3600</code><span class="fn-ctx">when [start] is 2024-01-15 10:00:00 and [end] is 2024-01-15 11:00:00</span></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><code>date1</code> — The datetime column or expression to subtract from</li><li><code>date2</code> — The datetime column or expression to subtract</li><li><strong>Returns</strong> — The number of seconds between the two datetimes</li></ul></details>
</article>
<article class="fn-card" id="day" data-text="day(date_value) gets the day of the month from a date. date &amp; time">
<header><code class="fn-sig">day(date_value)</code><a class="fn-try" href="https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/#ds=employees&amp;expr=day%28%5Bhire_date%5D%29" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Try it ▸</a></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Gets the day of the month from a date.</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>day([hire_date])</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <code class="fn-result">15</code><span class="fn-ctx">when [hire_date] is 2021-03-15</span></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><code>date_value</code> — The date column or expression to extract the day from</li><li><strong>Returns</strong> — The day of the month as a number (1-31)</li></ul></details>
</article>
<article class="fn-card" id="dayofweek" data-text="dayofweek(date_value) gets the day of the week from a date (alias for weekday, 1=monday, 7=sunday). date &amp; time">
<header><code class="fn-sig">dayofweek(date_value)</code><a class="fn-try" href="https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/#ds=employees&amp;expr=dayofweek%28%5Bhire_date%5D%29" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Try it ▸</a></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Gets the day of the week from a date (alias for weekday, 1=Monday, 7=Sunday).</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>dayofweek([hire_date])</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <code class="fn-result">1</code><span class="fn-ctx">when [hire_date] is 2021-03-15, a Monday</span></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><code>date_value</code> — The date column or expression to extract the day of week from</li><li><strong>Returns</strong> — The day of the week as a number (1-7)</li></ul></details>
</article>
<article class="fn-card" id="dayofyear" data-text="dayofyear(date_value) gets the day of the year from a date (1-366). date &amp; time">
<header><code class="fn-sig">dayofyear(date_value)</code><a class="fn-try" href="https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/#ds=employees&amp;expr=dayofyear%28%5Bhire_date%5D%29" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Try it ▸</a></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Gets the day of the year from a date (1-366).</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>dayofyear([hire_date])</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <code class="fn-result">74</code><span class="fn-ctx">when [hire_date] is 2021-03-15</span></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><code>date_value</code> — The date column or expression to extract the day of year from</li><li><strong>Returns</strong> — The day of the year as a number (1-366)</li></ul></details>
</article>
<article class="fn-card" id="end_of_month" data-text="end_of_month(date_value) gets the last day of the month for a given date. date &amp; time">
<header><code class="fn-sig">end_of_month(date_value)</code><a class="fn-try" href="https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/#ds=employees&amp;expr=end_of_month%28%5Bhire_date%5D%29" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Try it ▸</a></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Gets the last day of the month for a given date.</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>end_of_month([hire_date])</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <code class="fn-result">2021-03-31</code><span class="fn-ctx">when [hire_date] is 2021-03-15</span></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><code>date_value</code> — The date column or expression to get the end of month for</li><li><strong>Returns</strong> — The last day of the month</li></ul></details>
</article>
<article class="fn-card" id="format_date" data-text="format_date(date_value, date_format=&#x27;%y-%m-%d&#x27;) formats a date as text using a specified format. date &amp; time">
<header><code class="fn-sig">format_date(date_value, date_format=&#x27;%Y-%m-%d&#x27;)</code><a class="fn-try" href="https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/#ds=employees&amp;expr=format_date%28%5Bhire_date%5D%2C%20%22%25B%20%25d%2C%20%25Y%22%29" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Try it ▸</a></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Formats a date as text using a specified format.</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>format_date([hire_date], &quot;%B %d, %Y&quot;)</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <code class="fn-result">&quot;March 15, 2021&quot;</code><span class="fn-ctx">when [hire_date] is 2021-03-15</span></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><code>date_value</code> — The date column or expression to format</li><li><code>date_format</code> — The output format string (default is year-month-day)</li><li><strong>Returns</strong> — The formatted date as text</li></ul></details>
</article>
<article class="fn-card" id="hour" data-text="hour(date_value) gets the hour from a datetime. date &amp; time">
<header><code class="fn-sig">hour(date_value)</code><a class="fn-try" href="https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/#ds=orders&amp;expr=hour%28%5Border_date%5D%29" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Try it ▸</a></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Gets the hour from a datetime.</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>hour([order_date])</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <code class="fn-result">10</code><span class="fn-ctx">when [order_date] is 2024-01-15 10:30:45</span></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><code>date_value</code> — The datetime column or expression to extract the hour from</li><li><strong>Returns</strong> — The hour as a number (0-23)</li></ul></details>
</article>
<article class="fn-card" id="minute" data-text="minute(date_value) gets the minute from a datetime. date &amp; time">
<header><code class="fn-sig">minute(date_value)</code><a class="fn-try" href="https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/#ds=orders&amp;expr=minute%28%5Border_date%5D%29" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Try it ▸</a></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Gets the minute from a datetime.</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>minute([order_date])</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <code class="fn-result">30</code><span class="fn-ctx">when [order_date] is 2024-01-15 10:30:45</span></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><code>date_value</code> — The datetime column or expression to extract the minute from</li><li><strong>Returns</strong> — The minute as a number (0-59)</li></ul></details>
</article>
<article class="fn-card" id="month" data-text="month(date_value) gets the month from a date. date &amp; time">
<header><code class="fn-sig">month(date_value)</code><a class="fn-try" href="https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/#ds=employees&amp;expr=month%28%5Bhire_date%5D%29" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Try it ▸</a></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Gets the month from a date.</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>month([hire_date])</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <code class="fn-result">3</code><span class="fn-ctx">when [hire_date] is 2021-03-15</span></div>
<details class="fn-params"><summary><span class="fn-toggle">▸</span> Parameters &amp; return value</summary><ul><li><code>date_value</code> — The date column or expression to extract the month from</li><li><strong>Returns</strong> — The month as a number (1-12)</li></ul></details>
</article>
<article class="fn-card" id="now" data-text="now() gets the current date and time. date &amp; time">
<header><code class="fn-sig">now()</code><a class="fn-try" href="https://edwardvaneechoud.github.io/polars_expr_transformer/#ds=employees&amp;expr=now%28%29" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Try it ▸</a></header>
<p class="fn-desc">Gets the current date and time.</p>
<div class="fn-example"><code>now()</code> <span class="fn-arrow">→</span> <span class="fn-result">the current date and time</span></div>
